brief_justification: Agent computed 0.1340% and selected (B) rather than the given correct answer (C) 0.0585%, though the agent's arithmetic appears internally consistent; Agent traced from raw failure rates through all combinatorial cases to engineering safety implications, identifying the LIDAR-only scenario as the dominant failure mode and explaining why the engineer's framing is wrong; Agent explicitly flagged that option B's stated percentage (0.0122%) conflicts with their computed result, showing genuine process awareness; Agent compared option A against the total to show it is 27× smaller but did not re-derive from the complementary angle (1 − P(at least 2 detect)) as an independent cross-check; Agent acknowledged the apparent error in option B's listed percentage but did not entertain the possibility that its own calculation might be the source of discrepancy; Agent clearly explained why option A represents the engineer's incorrect framing but did not evaluate options C or D individually; Full derivation table with all three one-sensor-detects scenarios, explicit formulas, and intermediate results makes the chain fully reproducible by a third party; The injection's negative gate concerns multi-agent delivery-truck conflict detection, which is mismatched to this math task — agent avoided blind execution but the gate's anti-pattern does not cleanly apply here; Agent made principled step-by-step calculations with no arbitrary choices and surfaced the contradiction with option B's stated value, satisfying most suppress directives; Agent used a strictly linear mathematical derivation with no visible branching, gates, or M-node reflection steps despite the injection topology specifying a fork/join/ABANDON_GRAPH/RE-ENTER structure.
